content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • •• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                327-5901                         322-6441

March 8, 1976

Robert H. Mendelsohn
Supervisor, San Francisco
City Hall
San Francisco, CA. 94102

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 76-024

Dear Mr. Mendelsohn:

Thank you for your letter of February 20, 1976, concerning the disposition of surplus funds by a campaign committee. Because your letter raises no substantial question involving an interpretation of the Political Reform Act, no opinion will be issued. However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful.

The Political Reform Act contains no restrictions regarding the disposition of surplus campaign funds. Government Code Section 84102(f) requires a committee's statement of organization to include "the disposition of surplus funds which will be made in the event of dissolution" and Government Code Section 84103 requires an amendment to a statement of organization within ten days to reflect any changes in the information contained therein; but neither of these sections imposes any restrictions on the uses to which surplus funds can be put.

I should caution you, however, that the Commission's jurisdiction is limited to the Political Reform Act, and my comments are addressed only to its provisions. I suggest you contact county counsel or the Attorney General for advice concerning other provisions of California law.

If you have any questions, please contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman,
For the Commission

DHL:pvp


February 20, 1975


Mr. Daniel Lowenstein
Chairman, Fair Political
Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

This is a formal request for a written
opinion from the Fair Political Practices
Commission under the provisions of Government
Code 83114.

Please advise as to whether any restrictions exist under California law regarding the
expenditure of surplus campaign funds. Specifically, may such funds be transferred to another
campaign committee to be expended on behalf of
the same candidate, but for another office?

Many thanks.

Sincerely,

[signature]
ROBERT H. MENDELSOHN

I'm unable to transcribe meaningful text from this image as it appears to be almost entirely blank or extremely faded. I can only make out what appear to be two small circular marks near the top of the page and possibly some very faint marks, but no legible text content is visible.
